prompt:
We render onboarding flows from JSON schemas and every product team hand-rolls the paging logic differently. I want one headless wizard engine — no DOM, no framework, plain TypeScript on node (node --test, type stripping, zero deps). Three modules so the pieces stay testable on their own: fields.ts, schema.ts, wizard.ts.

A schema is { pages: [...] }. A page: { id, title?, showIf?, fields: [...] }. A field: { id, label?, type: 'text' | 'number' | 'boolean' | 'choice', required?, minLength?, maxLength?, pattern? (text), min?, max? (number), options? (choice) }. showIf is { field, equals: v } or { field, in: [v, ...] } and may only reference a field defined on an EARLIER page.

fields.ts — export validateField(field, value) -> string[] of human-readable problems, empty when fine. An undefined value is only a problem for required fields (say 'required' in the message) — otherwise skip all other checks. Otherwise check by type: text must be a string (mention text) and honor minLength/maxLength/pattern; number must be a real number, NaN excluded (mention number), honoring min/max; boolean must be true or false (mention boolean); choice must be one of options, and the rejected value must appear in the message. Bound violations must quote the bound. Collect EVERY violation, don't stop at the first.

schema.ts — export loadSchema(def), throwing on garbage, always naming the offender: zero pages; duplicate page ids; a page with no fields; a field id reused anywhere in the schema (answers are keyed flat by field id); a choice field without a non-empty options; an unknown field type; a showIf pointing at a field that doesn't exist or that doesn't live on an earlier page.

wizard.ts — export class Wizard, constructor takes the schema def (validate via loadSchema). current() -> snapshot of the current page. next(answers) submits the CURRENT page: keys that aren't this page's fields throw naming the key; submitted values MERGE over anything previously stored for the page (so revisiting and pressing on with {} just reuses the old answers); then every field on the page is validated — failures return { ok: false, errors: [{ field, messages }] } in page field order and nothing moves or is stored; success stores the merged answers and advances past any pages whose showIf is false under the stored answers, returning { ok: true, done } with done=true when nothing visible remains. When done, current() and next() throw (say complete). back() steps to the previous VISIBLE page keeping all answers; from the first page it throws; from the done state it reopens on the last visible page. pageAnswers(pageId) -> copy of the stored answers for any page (unknown page -> throw naming it) — a page hidden by a later answer change KEEPS its stored answers (they come back if it becomes visible again), they're just excluded from export(). export() -> flat object of the answered fields of currently-visible pages only, keys in schema order, and a copy every call.

The acceptance tests are the contract — node --test test_fields.ts test_schema.ts test_wizard.ts — and they stay as-is.
